symptoms.symptom_name: Inversion injury with lateral ATFL-region pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inversion mechanism with ATFL-region pain is typical of a lateral sprain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Able to weight-bear 4 steps (immediately and now)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to weight-bear four steps is an Ottawa criterion that lowers fracture probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony tenderness at Ottawa points

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bony tenderness at the malleoli/5th metatarsal/navicular makes fracture unlikely (Ottawa-negative).

symptoms.symptom_name: Point tenderness over ATFL, stable anterior drawer with firm endpoint (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Isolated ATFL tenderness with a stable joint supports a Grade 1 sprain but stability testing needs examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Imaging not indicated when Ottawa Ankle Rules are negative.
Only if bony tenderness or inability to weight-bear (Ottawa-positive) develops.
